channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Expedition Uncovers Sunken Ship Quest, Final Vessel of Ernest Shackleton
item.description: An expedition led by the Royal Canadian Geographical Society (RCGS) has captured the first images of the sunken ship Quest, the final vessel of Antarctic explorer Sir Ernest Shackleton. The ship, whic...

item.title: Humanity & AGI Summit 2026 Unites Global Leaders to Address AI's Physical Integration
item.description: The Humanity & AGI Summit 2026, organized by the AI Robotics Alliance of America (AIRA), convenes global leaders to discuss the societal implications of artificial intelligence (AI) as it transitions ...

item.title: Will Smith and Jada Pinkett Reunite After Years of Secret Separation
item.description: Will Smith and Jada Pinkett Smith have reportedly resumed living together after nearly seven years of secret separation. The couple quietly split in 2016 but kept their relationship private until 2023...

item.title: AmaZulu King Appoints New Spokesperson, Ending Thulani Zulu's Era
item.description: Prince Mpiyakhe Buthelezi has been officially appointed as the new spokesperson for King Misuzulu kaZwelithini of the AmaZulu royal family, marking the formal conclusion of Prince Thulani Zulu's tenur...

item.title: Ghana and South Africa Postpone Bilateral Talks Amid Anti-Migrant Tensions
item.description: Ghana has postponed planned bilateral meetings with South Africa scheduled for August, citing rising anti-migrant violence in the country. Government spokesperson Felix Kwakye Ofosu stated the violenc...

item.title: Learning to listen to my body after repeated injuries
item.description: The article recounts the author's childhood experiences of breaking their right arm seven times, initially attributing the incidents to bad luck. Over time, they realize the injuries were a signal to ...

item.title: Boeing Boosts Technical Support for Nigerian Airlines’ International Expansion
item.description: The article details how Boeing is pivoting its approach in Nigeria, moving from merely selling aircraft to providing comprehensive technical support and training for local airlines aiming to expand in...

item.title: Trump's Legal Misstep in Avoiding Payment to E. Jean Carroll
item.description: The article discusses a legal error made by Donald Trump's attorneys in their attempt to prevent E. Jean Carroll from receiving a court-ordered payment for sexual abuse. A legal expert, David Edwards,...

item.title: South African Soccer Teams Prepare for European Pre-Season Tours with High-Profile Matches
item.description: Kaizer Chiefs, Orlando Pirates, and Mamelodi Sundowns are embarking on European pre-season tours to strengthen their squads ahead of the new Betway Premiership season. Chiefs will train in Murcia, Spa...

item.title: Quebec's CAQ faces paradox of popular ideas and electoral decline
item.description: The article examines the political dilemma faced by Quebec's Coalition Avenir Québec (CAQ) as their right-leaning nationalist policies, once seen as revolutionary, now struggle to translate into elect...

item.title: Top Waterproof Makeup Products for Summer
item.description: This article highlights six essential waterproof makeup products ideal for summer, offering long-lasting wear against sweat and humidity. It recommends Urban Decay's Face Bond Waterproof Foundation as...

item.title: Defense Argues No Evidence Links Sara Duterte to Assassin Hiring in Marcos Case

item.title: Switzerland's Historic World Cup Run Enters Quarter-Finals
item.description: Switzerland made history by advancing to the World Cup quarter-finals for the first time since 1954, defeating Colombia 4-3 on penalties after a goalless draw in Vancouver. The victory sparked nationw...

item.title: Tanzania experiences calm day as July 7 passes without protests
item.description: Calm prevailed across Tanzania on July 7, 2026, as citizens continued daily activities without major incidents. Despite social media rumors about potential protests, police maintained visible security...

item.title: Trump Mistakenly References 'Islamic Republic of Japan' During NATO Summit
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump made a notable error during a NATO summit meeting with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy in Ankara, Turkey, on July 8, 2026. While discussing recent security threats,...

item.title: FilOil Cargo's Leadership Secures Letran's Eighth Win; San Beda Claims Second Seed
item.description: The article highlights a significant milestone in the UAAP basketball season as FilOil Cargo led Letran to their eighth consecutive victory, solidifying their dominance in the league. San Beda Univers...

item.title: Tanzania's Colonial Heritage in European Museums and the Fight for Cultural Ownership
item.description: The article discusses how Tanzania's colonial heritage, including artifacts and historical records, remains scattered across European museums and private collections. A Slovenian exhibition, 'Tanzania...

item.title: Access Blocked: Instructions for Resolving Bot Detection Issue
item.description: News Corp Australia has implemented software to manage crawler bot traffic on their websites. Users who are not bots but encounter access issues should follow specific steps to resolve the problem. Th...

item.title: Trump Legal Team Refuses to Pay E. Jean Carroll $5 Million in Damages
item.description: President Donald Trump's legal team continues to refuse paying E. Jean Carroll the $5 million damages verdict awarded after a 2023 jury found him liable for sexual abuse and defamation. Despite the Su...

item.title: Deputy Governors Lead Northern Forum Amid Security and Economic Pressures
item.description: The Northern States Governors’ Forum (NSGF) held its meeting in Kaduna, with a significant number of deputy governors attending instead of the state governors. Out of the 19 northern states expected, ...
